person.name: Anselme IV de Busca, Marquess of Bosco

nameForms.fullText: Anselme IV de Busca, Marquess of Bosco

sibling.name: Anselme IV de Busca, Marquess of Bosco

zh.lifeSummary: Anselme IV de Busca, Marquess of Bosco是Hugues II de Bosco的兒子，於1115年出生。他至少有1個女兒。他去世於1152年享年37歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Anselme IV de Busca, Marquess of Bosco是Hugues II de Bosco的儿子，于1115年出生。他至少有1个女儿。他去世于1152年享年37岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Busca,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Bernd, Hans, Wolfgang, Klaus, Lorenz, Dieter, Erna, Ernst, Helmut, Meinrad.

German (also Büsch): topographic name for someone who lived by a thicket or wood, from Middle Low German, Middle High German busch ‘bush, copse’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word or Büsch.
Dutch: variant of   Bosch  .

variants.name: Buschman
meanings: Dutch: derivative of Busch with the extension -man.
Americanized form of German   Buschmann  .

variants.name: Busche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Gerhardt, Guenther, Liselotte, Otto.
German: variant (from an inflected form) of   Busch  .

variants.name: Buscher
meanings: North German (also Büscher): topographic name for someone who lived near a copse, from Middle Low German busch ‘copse’ (see Busch ) + the locative suffix -er, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word.
North German: occupational name for a butcher, from Middle Low German buscher ‘non-guild butcher and meat retailer’.
Dutch: variant of   Busscher   ‘lumberjack’. Compare   Busker  .

variants.name: Bush
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a bushy area or thicket, from Middle English bush(e) ‘bush’ (probably from Old Norse buskr, or an unrecorded Old English busc).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Busch  .
Americanized form of Czech and Slovak Buš, Búš (see Bus ).

variants.name: Busching
meanings: North German (also Büsching): patronymic or a topographic name from Busch .
German: from French Bouchain, a habitational name from a place so named (Nord).

variants.name: Bussmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Konrad, Egon, Ingeborg.
German and Swiss German: either a northern variant of   Busch   or a variant of   Buss  .

variants.name: Bosch
meanings: Dutch; German (also Bösch): topographic name for someone living near the woods, from Dutch bos(ch) ‘wood’, Middle Low German bosch ‘bushes, woods’. This surname is also found in France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Bosh and Dutch Vandenbosch .
South German (Bösch): see Boesch .
Germanized form of Sorbian Boš and Bož: from a pet form of a Slavic personal name beginning with Bo-, such as Bogusław (see Boguslawski ) and Bolesław (see Bolek ).

variants.name: Buschmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Gunther, Mathias, Oskar, Rainer, Siegfried, Wilhelm.
German: variant of Busch , with the addition of Middle High German man ‘man’.

nameMeanings.name: Anselme
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from ‘divinity’ + ‘helmet’. St Anselm of Piedmont was Archbishop of Canterbury in the late 11th and early 12th centuries. See Anselm .

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: Anselme IV de Busca, Marquess of Bosco was born in 1115 as the son of Hugues II de Bosco. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in 1152, at the age of 37.

fr.lifeSummary: Anselme IV de Busca, Marquess of Bosco est né en 1115, fils de Hugues II de Bosco. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1152, à l’âge de 37 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Anselme IV de Busca, Marquess of Bosco wurde 1115, als Sohn von Hugues II de Bosco geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1152 im Alter von 37 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Anselme IV de Busca, Marquess of Bosco è nato il nel 1115 come figlio di Hugues II de Bosco. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1152, all'età di 37 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Anselme IV de Busca, Marquess of Boscoは1115年にHugues II de Boscoの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1152年に37歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Anselme IV de Busca, Marquess of Bosco(은)는 1115년 Hugues II de Bosco의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1152년 37세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Anselme IV de Busca, Marquess of Bosco nasceu em 1115 filho de Hugues II de Bosco. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1152, com 37 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Anselme IV de Busca, Marquess of Bosco родился в 1115 г. Его отца звали Hugues II de Bosco. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1152 г. в возрасте 37 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Anselme IV de Busca, Marquess of Bosco nació en 1115 como hijo de Hugues II de Bosco. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1152, a la edad de 37 años.
